required_skills: Distributed Systems Theory and CAP Theorem
Consensus Algorithms such as Raft and Paxos
preferred_skills: Distributed Transaction Management and Two-Phase Commit
Time Series and Append-Only Storage Systems
responsibilities: Architect and implement fault-tolerant distributed systems that maintain consistency, availability, and partition tolerance across geographically distributed data centers
Design consensus and leader election mechanisms using Raft or Paxos to coordinate state across distributed nodes without single points of failure
Build high-throughput event streaming pipelines using Apache Kafka to support real-time data propagation across hundreds of microservices
Implement distributed caching, data sharding, and replication strategies to achieve horizontal scalability and sub-millisecond read latencies at petabyte scale
Develop idempotent, retry-safe service interfaces with circuit breakers, bulkheads, and exponential backoff to handle partial failures gracefully
Design and enforce data consistency models including strong consistency, read-your-writes, and eventual consistency appropriate to each service's SLA requirements
Instrument distributed services with correlated trace IDs using Jaeger or OpenTelemetry to enable end-to-end latency profiling across service boundaries
Conduct load testing and chaos experiments using fault injection to validate system resilience under node failures, network partitions, and traffic spikes
Write detailed system design specifications and architectural decision records documenting trade-offs in consistency, availability, and operational complexity
Mentor junior engineers on distributed systems design patterns including saga orchestration, outbox pattern, and event sourcing for reliable state management
